title: Maze Product Outcomes, Repository Baseline, Accessibility, and Evidence · Program Brief: Repository Baseline
summary: A team says “build a maze solver” without defining the player task, puzzle rules, assistance, supported input, repository state, or proof of success. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the repository baseline increment in player, repository, accessibility, and evidence charter. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidenc…
objectives: Inspect revision, status, environment, dependencies, licenses, existing tests, and recovery anchors before changing the project.
Define player, task, puzzle rules, success, failure, session length, and observable value before selecting algorithms.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.title: Worked case pattern

Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the maze charter on player value, repository evidence, accessibility, and honest native limits; all graph claims need an independent witness; then change display scale and retain focus and control access. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. content.code:
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-outcomes-repo-accessibility-4
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-outcomes-repo-accessibility-4.
# Case 1655: a classroom algorithm comparison.
# Operating constraint: native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution.
# Changed case: change display scale and retain focus and control access.
def worked_maze_outcomes_repo_accessibility_4(player_task: str = "compare-solvers", acceptance_cases: int = 8, repository_clean: bool = True, accessibility_plan: bool = True, native_limits_named: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "gridworked4",
        "topology1655",
        "search4dekrow",
        "eventworked41655",
        "access1655worked4",
        "recovery4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if not player_task.strip() or acceptance_cases < 4:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "undefined-outcome"
    if not repository_clean:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "repository-baseline"
    if not accessibility_plan:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "accessibility-gap"
    if not native_limits_named:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "native-overclaim"
    assert acceptance_cases >= 4
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-maze-charter"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python maze decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected grid, topology, frontier, path, callback, state, accessibility, save, package, or recovery case, then state which Tcl, Tk, tkinter, Canvas, display, focus, input, assistive-technology, filesystem, timing, installer, or production claim still needs controlled native transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: maze-repository-baseline-1be6av
# Competency: maze-repository-baseline.
# Case 9778: a museum kiosk accessibility review.
# Operating constraint: all graph claims need an independent witness.
# Changed case: change display scale and retain focus and control access.
def evidence_maze_repository_baseline_1be6av(player_task: str = "compare-solvers", acceptance_cases: int = 8, repository_clean: bool = True, accessibility_plan: bool = True, native_limits_named: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1be6av = "1be6av-va6eb1-9778"
    assert evidence_variant_1be6av
    evidence_axes_1be6av = (
        "grid1be6av",
        "topology9778",
        "searchva6eb1",
        "event1be6av9778",
        "access97781be6av",
        "recoveryva6eb19778",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1be6av)) == 6
    if not player_task.strip() or acceptance_cases < 4:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "undefined-outcome"
    if not repository_clean:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "repository-baseline"
    if not accessibility_plan:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "accessibility-gap"
    if not native_limits_named:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "native-overclaim"
    assert acceptance_cases >= 4
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-maze-charter"

steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Repository Baseline model · Theory Repository Baseline, keep Repository Baseline connected to executable Python grid, topology, search, event, state, accessibility, package, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Inspect revision, status, environment, dependencies, licenses, existing tests, and recovery anchors before changing the project.

hints: For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Repository Baseline model · Theory Repository Baseline, name the player, revision, Python, Tcl and Tk versions, grid, coordinate, seed, topology, frontier, path, event, state, render, accessibility, package, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Repository Baseline precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Repository Baseline model · Theory Repository Baseline, trace this concrete input and predict coordinate, wall, topology, frontier, route, callback, transition, render, accessibility, failure, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory puzzle team handles case 1462 during a museum kiosk accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the maze charter on player value, repository evidence, accessibility, and honest native limits; the prior good state must survive failure; then change display scale and retain focus and control access. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Inspect revision, status, environment, dependencies, licenses, existing tests, and recovery anchors before changing the project.

For Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend the Repository Baseline model · Theory Repository Baseline, challenge the documented misconception with changed-grid, seed, wall, generator, solver, weight, heuristic, callback, state, focus, scale, save, package, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: A small learning project does not need repository or dependency evidence.
